---
title: "EEG & Signal Processing"
subtitle: "An Interactive Tutorial for Future Physicians"
---

## Welcome

You're training to become a doctor. At some point — maybe in neurology, maybe in the ICU, maybe in the OR — you will look at a screen showing electrical signals from a patient's brain, heart, or muscles.

This tutorial will teach you to **read those signals**. Not just glance at them — actually understand what you're seeing.

## How This Works

Each module is **interactive**. You'll drag sliders, change parameters, and watch signals respond in real time. There are short quiz questions along the way — these are **not graded**. They're just checkpoints to make sure the concepts are landing.

**Module 8 is graded.** That's where you'll get real EEG data and find out what's going on with the patient.

## Modules

```{=html}
<style>
.module-grid { display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill, minmax(220px, 1fr)); gap:14px; margin:1.2em 0; }
.module-card { display:block; padding:16px 18px; border-radius:10px; border:1px solid #d8dde3; background:#fafbfc; color:#2c3e50; text-decoration:none; transition:all 0.15s; }
.module-card:hover { border-color:#2980b9; background:#fff; box-shadow:0 3px 10px rgba(41,128,185,0.12); transform:translateY(-1px); }
.module-card.disabled { opacity:0.55; cursor:not-allowed; background:#f0f2f5; }
.module-card.disabled:hover { border-color:#d8dde3; box-shadow:none; transform:none; }
.module-number { font-size:0.78em; font-weight:700; letter-spacing:0.08em; color:#8e44ad; margin-bottom:2px; }
.module-title { font-size:1.08em; font-weight:700; color:#2c3e50; margin-bottom:4px; }
.module-desc { font-size:0.85em; color:#555; line-height:1.4; }
.module-card.challenge .module-number { color:#e67e22; }
.module-card.challenge .module-title { color:#c0392b; }
.module-soon { display:inline-block; font-size:0.7em; font-weight:700; letter-spacing:0.05em; color:#e67e22; background:#fdf2e3; padding:1px 6px; border-radius:4px; margin-left:6px; vertical-align:middle; }
</style>
<div class="module-grid">
  <a class="module-card" href="modules/01_signals.qmd"><div class="module-number">01</div><div class="module-title">Signals</div><div class="module-desc">Amplitude, frequency, phase — the language of bioelectrical recordings.</div></a>
  <a class="module-card" href="modules/02_sampling.qmd"><div class="module-number">02</div><div class="module-title">Sampling</div><div class="module-desc">How continuous biology becomes numbers in a computer.</div></a>
  <a class="module-card" href="modules/03_filtering.qmd"><div class="module-number">03</div><div class="module-title">Filtering</div><div class="module-desc">Stripping noise without throwing away the signal.</div></a>
  <a class="module-card" href="modules/04_fft.qmd"><div class="module-number">04</div><div class="module-title">FFT</div><div class="module-desc">Decomposing any signal into its hidden frequency ingredients.</div></a>
  <a class="module-card" href="modules/05_eeg_basics.qmd"><div class="module-number">05</div><div class="module-title">EEG Basics</div><div class="module-desc">Electrodes, the 10-20 system, and the clinical frequency bands.</div></a>
  <a class="module-card" href="modules/05b_montages.qmd"><div class="module-number">05B</div><div class="module-title">Montages &amp; Dipoles</div><div class="module-desc">How the same brain signal looks different under different display montages.</div></a>
  <a class="module-card" href="modules/06_artifacts.qmd"><div class="module-number">06</div><div class="module-title">Artifacts</div><div class="module-desc">Blinks, muscle, ECG, line noise — distinguishing brain from contamination.</div></a>
  <a class="module-card" href="modules/07_spectrograms.qmd"><div class="module-number">07</div><div class="module-title">Spectrograms</div><div class="module-desc">Watching brain rhythms evolve — frequency content as a movie in time.</div></a>
  <a class="module-card challenge" href="modules/08_challenge.qmd"><div class="module-number">08</div><div class="module-title">The Challenge</div><div class="module-desc">A real EEG, a real clinical question. The graded capstone.</div></a>
</div>
```

## What You'll Need

- A web browser (you're using one right now — good start)
- For Module 8: Python or MATLAB, and a willingness to ask an AI for help writing code

## Homework

A self-paced problem set covering modules 1–8, designed to be solved by reading the modules and playing with the widgets.

```{=html}
<div style="margin:1.2em 0;">
  <object data="homework/homework.pdf" type="application/pdf" width="100%" height="800px"
          style="border:1px solid #d8dde3; border-radius:8px;">
    <embed src="homework/homework.pdf" type="application/pdf" width="100%" height="800px" />
    <p style="padding:20px; text-align:center; color:#555;">
      Your browser can't display the PDF inline.
      <a href="homework/homework.pdf" download>Download it here</a>.
    </p>
  </object>
  <div style="margin-top:10px;">
    <a class="module-card" href="homework/homework.pdf" download
       style="display:inline-block; max-width:260px; background:#fff; border:1px solid #2980b9;">
      <div class="module-number" style="color:#2980b9;">PDF</div>
      <div class="module-title">Download Homework</div>
      <div class="module-desc">11 problems &middot; keep this site open while you work</div>
    </a>
  </div>
</div>
```

Let's begin.

::: {.module-nav}
[Begin Module 1 →](modules/01_signals.qmd)
:::
